title: Ways to Describe Emotions - DISGUST
description: Comprehensive guide to understanding disgust emotions, their triggers, manifestations, and effects

promptingEvents.description: Events that trigger feelings of disgust
events: Seeing or smelling human or animal waste products
Having a person or an animal that is dirty, slimy, or unclean come close to you
Tasting something or being forced to swallow something you really don't want
Seeing or being near a dead body
Touching items worn or owned by a stranger, dead person, or disliked person
Observing or hearing about a person who grovels or who strips another person of dignity
Observing or hearing about a person acting with extreme hypocrisy or fawning
Observing or hearing about betrayal, child abuse, racism, or other types of cruelty
Being forced to watch something that deeply violates your own Wise Mind values
Being confronted with someone who is deeply violating your own Wise Mind values
Being forced to engage in or watch unwanted sexual contact

interpretations.description: Interpretations of events that prompt feelings of disgust
interpretations: Believing that you are swallowing something toxic
Your skin or your mind is being contaminated
Your own body or body parts are ugly
Others are evil or the 'scum' of the earth, or that they disrespect authority or the group
Disapproving of or feeling morally superior to another
Extreme disapproval of yourself or your own feelings, thoughts, or behaviors
Judging that a person is deeply immoral or has sinned or violated the natural order of things
Judging someone's body as extremely ugly

biologicalChanges.description: Biological changes and experiences of disgust
changes: Urge to vomit, vomiting, gagging, choking
Having a lump in your throat
Intense urge to destroy or get rid of something
Urge to run away or push away

behaviors: Pushing or kicking away; running away
Stepping over; crowding another person out
Physically attacking causes of your disgust
Speaking with a sarcastic voice tone
Nose and top lip tightened up; smirking

effects: Ruminating about the situation that's making you feel disgusted
